back_translation: And he arose and went, and Jonathan came into the city.
And David came to Nob, to Ahimelech the priest, and Ahimelech trembled to meet David, and said to him, 'Why are you alone, and no man is with you?'

And David said to Ahimelech the priest, 'The king has commanded me a matter, and said to me, Let no man know anything of the matter on which I am sending you and which I have commanded you. And I have appointed the young men to a certain unnamed place.
And now, what is under your hand? Five loaves of bread give in my hand, or whatever is found.'
And the priest answered David and said, 'There is no common bread under my hand, but if there is holy bread — provided the young men have kept themselves from women.'

And David answered the priest and said to him, 'Indeed, women have been kept from us as in times past when I went out, and the vessels of the young men have been holy, and this is a common journey, and how much more today shall it be holy in the vessel.'
And the priest gave him holy bread, for there was no bread there except the bread of the presence, which was removed from before the LORD, to put hot bread in its place on the day it was taken.
And there was a man of Saul's servants on that day, detained before the LORD, and his name was Doeg the Edomite, chief of the shepherds who belonged to Saul.
And David said to Ahimelech, 'Is there not under your hand a spear or sword? For neither my sword nor my weapons did I take in my hand, because the king's matter was urgent.'

And the priest said, 'The sword of Goliath the Philistine, whom you struck in the Valley of Elah — behold, it is wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod. If you will take it for yourself, take it; for there is no other except it here.' And David said, 'There is none like it; give it to me.'
And David arose and fled on that day from before Saul, and came to Achish king of Gath.
And the servants of Achish said to him, 'Is this not David, king of the land? Did they not answer in the dances, saying, Saul struck his thousands, and David his ten thousands?'
And David put these words in his heart and was very afraid before Achish king of Gath.
And he changed his behavior in their eyes and feigned madness in their hands, and scribbled on the doors of the gate, and let his saliva run down to his beard.
And Achish said to his servants, 'Behold, you see a man who is acting insane. Why do you bring him to me?
Am I lacking madmen, that you have brought this one to act insane before me? Shall this one come into my house?'
